GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "The latest entertaining trend? Dressing to match your dinner party", "Expert style advice for dining in or out", "Because staying in is the new going out, the at-home dinner party is the hottest ticket in town at the moment. So how do you inject the same sense of excitement into a living room soiree as you would derive from heading to the latest-and-greatest restaurant opening? By dressing to match your dinner table, of course. No really: by choosing a sartorially inspired mood - then dressing up to 'theme' and co-ordinating your table top with stylish servingware, glasses and decor - you'll inject a sense of fun into the night (or long afternoon) that your guests will feel the moment they walk in the door. This season, interiors and fashion go hand-in-hand, whether it be with high-summer pinks and citrus, an acid-hued rainbow aesthetic, or a refined palette of muted browns, black and gold accents. With a legacy that spans 35 years, Harrolds is Australia's truly luxury department store - and having recently launched global shipping, their edit of clothing, accessories and fragrance will be your go-to in time for party season. With over 100 brands, ranging from local heroes Christopher Esber, Albus Lumen and Sarah and Sebastian, to major international labels such as Bottega Veneta, Balenciaga, and Acne Studios - you'll find the piece for you, no matter the occasion. Before sunset. You might not be on a Spanish island, but your outfit doesn't have to know: this Wynn Hamlyn dress is decidedly party-ready, and a low heel by Manolo Blahnik (new to Harrolds) is the perfect pair to party all night in. Top your look with this micro bag by Gedebe - you're not going far, after all. Spritz this heady sandalwood scent by Creed around your space, then toast to a new season with glasses by Maison Balzac. Staying home has never been so much fun. After dark Whether it's date night or an intimate inner party with friends, keep things classic (but fashion-forward) in moody brown and noir hues, then accent with opulent glass- and table-ware. Scandi label Saks Potts' monogram print will bring a sense of cult cool, paired with statement leather pants from Petar Petrov. This beloved Cire Trudon scent, heavy in leather and tobacco notes, will set the mood. Buy now at harrolds.com.au Rainbow stylin' Tap into the of-the-moment rainbow trend with playful ombre pieces - such as this vibrant shirt dress by Christopher Esber, and statement-making scent by Amouage (with fresh notes of rose, ylang-ylang, orange, blossom and jasmine) - then top your table with contrasting hues to match. When it comes to this look, there are no rules, only to be guided by a strong sense of whimsy. Visit harrolds.com.au - or visit one of the luxury department store's Sydney, Melbourne and Gold Coast outposts - to discover their range of pieces for any occasion."
